title: rubus candicans subsp. neomalacus
intro: Rubus candicans subsp. neomalacus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan bramble, is a plant species belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to the Himalayas and parts of China. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Rubus candicans subsp. neomalacus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support digestive health. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in herbal remedies to address various health conditions.
